Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. TruffleRuby 88 upvotes, 4 comments
  2. From 300KB to 69KB per Token: How LLM Architectures Solve the KV Cache Problem 100 upvotes, 7 comments
  3. TinyLoRA – Learning to Reason in 13 Parameters 131 upvotes, 12 comments
  4. Learn Something Old Every Day, Part XVIII: How Does FPU Detection Work? 38 upvotes, 4 comments
  5. Inside the 'self-driving' lab revolution 26 upvotes, 2 comments
  6. Ministack (Replacement for LocalStack) 179 upvotes, 37 comments
  7. GitHub's Historic Uptime 444 upvotes, 109 comments
  8. 4D Doom 165 upvotes, 35 comments
  9. Bring Back MiniDV with This Raspberry Pi FireWire Hat 19 upvotes, 1 comments
  10. Show HN: Forkrun – NUMA-aware shell parallelizer (50×–400× faster than parallel) 125 upvotes, 30 comments
  11. Back to FreeBSD – Part 2 – Jails 65 upvotes, 13 comments
  12. Teenage Engineering's PO-32 acoustic modem and synth implementation 95 upvotes, 22 comments
  13. A Primer on Long-Duration Life Support 93 upvotes, 22 comments
  14. Open source CAD in the browser (Solvespace) 313 upvotes, 99 comments
  15. A dot a day keeps the clutter away 230 upvotes, 70 comments
  16. We intercepted the White House app's network traffic 185 upvotes, 56 comments
  17. Cohere Transcribe: Speech Recognition 175 upvotes, 54 comments
  18. The Claude Code Source Leak: fake tools, frustration regexes, undercover mode 1034 upvotes, 393 comments
  19. Show HN: Postgres extension for BM25 relevance-ranked full-text search 120 upvotes, 36 comments
  20. Ordinary Lab Gloves May Have Skewed Microplastic Data 83 upvotes, 23 comments
  21. Axios compromised on NPM – Malicious versions drop remote access trojan 1805 upvotes, 730 comments
  22. Neanderthals survived on a knife's edge for 350k years 73 upvotes, 22 comments
  23. Show HN: 1-Bit Bonsai, the First Commercially Viable 1-Bit LLMs 175 upvotes, 77 comments
  24. I Traced My Traffic Through a Home Tailscale Exit Node 101 upvotes, 42 comments
  25. Chess in SQL 5 upvotes, 0 comments
  26. OpenAI closes funding round at an $852B valuation 393 upvotes, 333 comments
  27. Use string views instead of passing std:wstring by const& 31 upvotes, 25 comments
  28. Analyzing Geekbench 6 under Intel's BOT 14 upvotes, 10 comments
  29. Slop is not necessarily the future 203 upvotes, 355 comments
